strategies.hypothesis: A single independent answer is the minimum-cost baseline.
strategies.name: Direct One

strategies.hypothesis: A small independent plurality is a stable low-cost baseline.
strategies.name: Five-Vote Plurality

strategies.hypothesis: A five-answer bank paired with the validated three-reviewer falsification mechanism may retain much of the champion's accuracy at eight calls per case rather than twelve; earlier five-solver repair systems reached 0.417–0.500 pooled accuracy.
strategies.name: Efficient Five-Bank Falsifying Repair

strategies.hypothesis: Blind regeneration raised candidate coverage on two panels but plurality failed to surface the new answers. Three blind fixed-lens regenerators now expand the bank, then one fresh falsifying integrator examines the shuffled unique augmented candidates and may repair them. This isolates candidate integration without changing the generation stage.
strategies.name: Blind Regeneration then Falsifying Integration

strategies.hypothesis: Replicate the unchanged call-efficiency probe. One reviewer matched three reviewers in Iteration 16 with the same correction balance and fewer calls, while its earlier pooled helpful-to-harmful record remains positive.
strategies.name: One-Review Five-Bank Falsifying Repair

strategies.hypothesis: Restore the established orthogonal sequential architecture. Passing one proposed answer and critique forward may concentrate falsification more coherently than independent reviewer plurality, and its prior positive corrections warrant replication on a fresh panel.
strategies.name: Sequential Five-Bank Cross-Examination
